ops.title: Before dispatching a migration/track lane, fetch and check origin/main for that surface — plans and local checkouts go stale across sessions
ops.body:
On 2026-07-18, Wave 1 Track A (cookiesync drops keyhelper → authkit) was dispatched per the approved plan and executed cleanly — but the entire migration had ALREADY landed on cookie-sync's origin/main on 07-17 (a different session shipped it and even cut the 0.15.0/0.16.0 releases). The plan's Status section and the local checkout were both two days stale. The duplicate lane cost a full opus run and a conflicted rebase that had to be abandoned wholesale.

**Why:** plans are written from one session's view; other sessions (or the user) land work on the same repos in parallel. The fleet's repos are shared working copies with independent activity.

**How to apply:** before dispatching any per-repo track from a plan, run `jj git fetch` in that repo and skim `origin/main`'s recent log for the track's surface (or have the lane's first step do it and return early on overlap). Treat a plan's "remaining work" claims about OTHER repos as hypotheses to verify, not facts. Related: [[parallelize-aggressively]] — fan out wide, but fetch first.
